title: QUIZ 10 - Hierarchy of AWS Global Infrastructure

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of an AWS Edge Location?
AWS Regions consist of:
Which AWS service uses Edge Locations to deliver cached content?
An Availability Zone is:
options: A geographically isolated data center within a Region
questions.answer: A geographically isolated data center within a Region

questions.question: The main purpose of Availability Zones is:
Which component is designed to reduce latency?
Latency means:
options: Time taken for data to travel between client and server
questions.answer: Time taken for data to travel between client and server

questions.question: CloudFront improves application performance by:
AWS Regions are:
The best Region for deploying an application is usually:
options: The first Region created by AWS

questions.question: Which statement about Availability Zones is TRUE?
options: They share the same power source.
They are physically isolated from each other.
They are used only for storage.
questions.answer: They are physically isolated from each other.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of caching?
Deploying an application across multiple Availability Zones provides:
Which AWS component is closest to end users?
Which of the following is an example of static content?
What is the relationship between Regions and Availability Zones?
Why should production applications avoid a single Availability Zone?
options: It creates a single point of failure.
questions.answer: It creates a single point of failure.

questions.question: Which AWS infrastructure component is specifically used by CloudFront?
A user in Asia accessing an application hosted only in the US may experience:
Which statement best summarizes AWS Global Infrastructure?
options: AWS uses a single data center for all customers.
AWS uses globally distributed Regions, Availability Zones, and Edge Locations to provide scalability, resilience, and low latency.
AWS only operates in North America.
AWS stores all customer data in one Region.
questions.answer: AWS uses globally distributed Regions, Availability Zones, and Edge Locations to provide scalability, resilience, and low latency.
